commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes npm-shrinkwrap.json act as npm package-install evidence and as the npm dependency lock marker for update/status detection, with tests for npm shrinkwrap and git/source precedence.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +71. Total +78 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows a package root with packageManager pnpm and npm-shrinkwrap.json is classified as pnpm before lockfiles are considered, matching the linked npm-status report.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can keep the npm shrinkwrap behavior while preserving documented pnpm/bun package-install detection and adding regression coverage.

target.title: fix(update): detect npm shrinkwrap package installs
target.body:
## Summary
- treat `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as npm install evidence for published package roots, even when package.json carries build-time `packageManager: pnpm`
- keep packageManager precedence for git/source roots that also include shrinkwrap
- use `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as the npm dependency lock marker when present so `openclaw status --json` does not report a missing pnpm/package-lock marker

Fixes #87732.

## Surface area
- No public config changes.
- No plugin API or plugin contract changes.
- No update command behavior rewrite; this only changes status/install detection and dependency marker reporting.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
A package-style OpenClaw install that contains `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, `node_modules`, and a build-time `packageManager: pnpm` declaration should be detected as an npm package install. `openclaw status --json` should not report missing pnpm dependency markers for that shape.

Real environment tested:
WSL2 Ubuntu checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-proof-87778-shrinkwrap`, PR head `9303050154caca99396916f79158995d2d4fac4d`, source-level temp package root using the real `detectPackageManager` and `checkUpdateStatus` code paths.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Built a temp package root with `package.json` declaring `packageManager: pnpm@11.2.2`, `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, and `node_modules`.
2. Ran `detectPackageManager(root)` against that temp root.
3. Ran `checkUpdateStatus({ root, includeRegistry: false, timeoutMs: 1000 })` against that temp root.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "status": "pass",
  "rootSuffix": "openclaw-87778-proof-b7PoRq",
  "detected": "npm",
  "installKind": "package",
  "packageManager": "npm",
  "deps": {
    "manager": "npm",
    "status": "ok",
    "lockfile": "npm-shrinkwrap.json",
    "marker": "node_modules"
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The detector returned `npm`, and update status reported a package install with npm deps, `status: ok`, `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as the lockfile, and `node_modules` as the marker.

What was not tested:
No registry lookup was tested; this proof disables registry access with `includeRegistry: false` because the bug is local install/dependency-marker detection.

## Validation
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts src/infra/update-check.test.ts src/commands/status.update.test.ts src/commands/status-json-payload.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 4 files, 36 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/detect-package-manager.ts src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts src/infra/update-check.ts src/infra/update-check.test.ts src/commands/status.update.test.ts src/commands/status-json-payload.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json src/infra/detect-package-manager.ts src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts src/infra/update-check.ts src/infra/update-check.test.ts src/commands/status.update.test.ts src/commands/status-json-payload.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`
